title: sesamoides pygmaea var. sesamoides
intro: Sesamoides pygmaea var. sesamoides, also known as Dwarf Sea Rosemary or Little Sea Rosemary, is a small, shrub-like plant that belongs to the family Campanulaceae. It is native to the coastal regions of Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating various health issues such as wounds, infections, and digestive problems. Sesamoides pygmaea var. sesamoides can be prepared as a tea or infused oil, and it is also used in herbal supplements to promote overall health and well-being.
